source_fact: assignment_gaifman_close_mono proves the forward close direction: r ≤ s and close at r imply close at s.
blocked_claim: Blocked: The earlier radius monotonicity shape r ≤ s and LocalityInput r imply LocalityInput s is not directly derivable from the current locality surface, because applying an r-radius invariant to an s-radius close hypothesis would require the reverse close direction.
admissible_replacement_direction: s ≤ r and LocalityInput r imply LocalityInput s is the directly admissible weakening direction for locality input surfaces.
claims: The current forward assignment-close monotonicity direction is recorded.
The larger-radius locality input lift is classified as blocked under the current definition.
The admissible replacement direction is smaller-radius weakening of the locality input surface.
nonclaims: Does not prove radius monotonicity of locality input surfaces.
Does not prove reverse assignment-close monotonicity.
Does not prove max-radius conjunction closure.
Does not prove max-radius disjunction closure.
Does not prove existential quantifier handling.
Does not prove arbitrary formula-radius construction.
Does not prove Gaifman locality for unguarded first-order logic.
Does not prove general finite model theory closure.
